problem:
Bug: PostgresStore numeric filter operators ($gt, $gte, $lt, $lte) use lexicographic (text) comparison instead of numeric comparison. `PostgresStore._get_filter_condition()` uses `value->>key` (text extraction)
with `str(value)` for `$gt`, `$gte`, `$lt`, `$lte` operators.
Since `->>` returns TEXT in PostgreSQL, numeric comparisons use lexicographic
ordering instead of numeric ordering, producing silently wrong query results.

Expected: `filter={"score": {"$gte": 10}}` returns only items where sc (attempted fix: --skip-validation "Skip all validation steps")
memory_hint: Do not use --skip-validation. It is a hallucinated flag. Use --help to verify.
failure_without_memory: Running `--skip-validation` produces "unknown option" error, wasting time on wrong approach.
